steps.title: Set up the discriminant condition for two real solutions
steps.content:
Shortcut (discriminant): For $rx^2 - 28sx + 7r = 0$ to have two distinct real solutions, $\Delta > 0$. With $a=r$, $b=-28s$, $c=7r$:
$(-28s)^2 - 4(r)(7r) > 0$
$784s^2 - 28r^2 > 0$
$28s^2 > r^2$, i.e., $r^2 < 28s^2$

steps.title: Find the minimum value of r/s
steps.content:
$\left|\dfrac{r}{s}\right| < \sqrt{28} = 2\sqrt{7}$
Since $r > 0$ and $s < 0$, the ratio $r/s$ is negative, so $r/s > -2\sqrt{7}$.
The minimum value of $k$ is $-2\sqrt{7} \approx -5.29$. The answer is -2√7.
